sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_data (id INT PRIMARY KEY, num1 FLOAT, num2 FLOAT, xml_col XML);
INSERT INTO test_data (id, num1, num2, xml_col) VALUES (1, 10.0, 20.0, 'Value1');
INSERT INTO test_data (id, num1, num2, xml_col) VALUES (2, NULL, 30.0, 'Value2');
INSERT INTO test_data (id, num1, num2, xml_col) VALUES (3, 40.0, 50.0, 'Value3');
INSERT INTO test_data (id, num1, num2, xml_col) VALUES (4, 0.0, 0.0, 'Value4');
SELECT CORR(num1, num2) AS correlation_value, xpath_string('/root/a', xml_col) AS extracted_value FROM test_data WHERE num1 IS NOT NULL GROUP BY extracted_value HAVING correlation_value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_data WHERE CORR(num1, xpath_string('/root/a', xml_col)::FLOAT) IS NOT NULL;
SELECT AVG(num1) AS avg_num1, MAX(CORR(num1, num2)) AS max_corr FROM test_data GROUP BY (xpath_string('/root/a', xml_col));
SELECT num1, num2, xpath_string('/root/b', xml_col) AS xml_value FROM test_data WHERE CORR(num1, num2) > 0.5 ORDER BY num1 DESC;
SELECT id, CORR(num1, num2) OVER (PARTITION BY xpath_string('/root/a', xml_col)) AS corr_partition FROM test_data;
